events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a trench approximately 6 feet deep with vertical walls, no protective system (trench box, shoring, or sloping), spoil piled within 1 foot of the edge, and no ladder visible. This is a clear OSHA 1926 Subpart P violation. Trenches 5 feet or deeper require a protective system unless excavated in stable rock. Spoil must be kept at least 2 feet from the edge. Adequate means of egress (ladder, ramp, etc.) must be provided for trenches 4 feet or deeper.

Decision: fail
Risk: critical
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: non-compliant
Percent complete: estimate maybe 30
Findings: no protective system, spoil too close to edge, no means of egress
Actions: stop work, install protective system, remove spoil from edge, provide ladder
References: OSHA 1926 Subpart P (1926.652, 1926.651)

    "Provide a ladder, ramp, or stairway extending at least 3 feet above the trench edge and spaced no more than 25 feet laterally",
    "Conduct daily inspection by a competent person before workers re-enter the excavation"

Work must not continue until these controls are in place.",
